comment.body:
we never been rich, my family was always hard-working,
i also worked during summers, even during school years... 
my father is a lumberjack, i helped him many times... i loved chopping trees.
i like to work... i don't like to just sit there and seeing the time passing by...

i made myself rich by lot of work, lot of money saving and investing, studying etc.
yes i worked for a bank too and a private fund as an enterpreneur, and outside kind of "advisor", but i never wanted to work in finance. 
i get a lot of job offers from NY through ABD to J'lem, but i don't take any of them... i never wanted to be a part of that white-collar crimial sphere...

i had money even before turned to 30s.
And i bought expensive stuffs for myself.
but only if it had quality, actual value (i mean in an aesthetical sense)... for example i love fragrances... and italian tayloring. 
but you would never see any logo on me.

once when I was 14 yo i was in Budapest alone.
a trainticked costed only a few hunders forint back then, and i just didn't had the money when i wanted to go back home by train.
i think it was 10-20 forint that I missed, dunno, but relatively a small amount. 
and the cashier did not give me ticket... 
yes, for a 14yo girl being alone in a big city.
i had to ask strangers if they can give me money.
i never felt that ashamed in my life than when i did this... (of course i got some "if you suck me" and stuff, so i had to ask multiple ppl)

and this experience is very deeply in my mind.
i know how much 1 euro worths.
i know what it feels like when you miss 50 cent and you can't call help, you can't buy the food, or a ticket to go home... I experienced it.
so i value money, and i don't spend on unnecessary things... especially not on things that are MEANT TO BE flashy and show-offy in order to make others feel how loosers they are.
I'm just like my father in this sense. He was very poor in his childhood and was humiliated many times, but he fought and didn't let himself to be humiliated. He has dignity. 
and ppl who spend money on items thats only purpose is to humiliate the so-called "loosers" makes me angry sometimes... (even tho it is a personal thing if you feel so or not, but those items are meant to have that purpose. thats the key)

comment.body:
and it wasn't a terrible thing.
but my father made me know what money worths.
when i first went with him to the forest, i was 12 yo, i ate the bread we bought differently...
even tho my job was only to throw the chopped trees onto the truck, then down when we drove to the dropoff location... i saw what work he is doing for us...
and i always prayed in secret to God to give me opportunity to be able to payback everything for my parents...
and when I first took them into a holiday, it was a wonderful feeling..

and i always prayed in secret to God to give me opportunity to be able to payback everything for my parents...
and when I first took them into a holiday, it was a wonderful feeling..

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
they were sooo shy, but we talked a lot...
bcs you know it feels a bit frustrating for a father that his daughter takes him to holiday and pays for everything... but i said: and you? how much hour did you work for me to give me clothes, food, school, PC, to take me onto vacation, etc... 
i said: if you every feel this frustration, just remind to this what i'm telling you, it's not even the 1/4 of what you gave me :))

of course, it is limited what i can give them, bsc they don't accept anything easily
